title: ranunculus alismifolius var. lemmonii
intro: Ranunculus alismifolius var. lemmonii, also known as Lemmon's buttercup, is a rare and endemic plant species found in the southwestern United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing and reduce pain and swelling. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, due to its expectorant properties. Traditionally, Lemmon's buttercup has been prepared as an herbal tea or infusion, and its roots have been used to make a topical salve or ointment to treat various health conditions.
